title: Manettia dubia
taxonomy: Manettia dubia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the madder family. This family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes coffee, gardenias, and jasmine. Manettia dubia is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a shrub or small tree with clusters of small, fragrant flowers. Within the Rubiaceae family, Manettia dubia is placed in the genus Manettia, which includes about 50 species of flowering plants.

intro: Manettia dubia, also known as "Wild Jamaica" or "Scarlet Willow," is a flowering plant species in the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of the Caribbean and Central America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Manettia dubia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Manettia dubia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of a tincture or extract, making it a valued ingredient in traditional remedies.
